Title: Introduction to IP with Ian Drew
Content:
In this video provided by Ian Drew from Davies Collison Cave the various different aspects of intellectual property are introduced along with why they are important to your business.

Keywords: Davies Collison Cave, Ian Drew, Intellectual Property, Trademark, Patent, Designs, Copyright, Confidential Information, Domain Name, Business Name, Company Name, IP

Title: Trade Marks with Sally Foreman
Content:
In this video provided by Sally Foreman from Davies Collison Cave the importance of identifying your trademarks and protecting them appropriately is discussed. Trademarks are becoming a more valuable 'intangible' asset that business invest resources in developing and can become an important component in the perceived business value.

Keywords: Davies Collison Cave, Sally Foreman, Trademark, Trade Mark, Protection, Registration, Distinctive, Intellectual Property, IP

Keywords: Davies Collison Cave, Claire Gregg, Richard Brown, Patent, Design Right, Registered Design, Infringement, Protection, Intellectual Property, IP, Claims

Title: Brand Strategy- The Foundations
Content:
Presented by The Offices, this module explains how to create the foundations of an impactful brand, with a discussion of four critical areas: (a) purpose statement, (b) unique selling proposition, (c) brand values and (d) market position. It forms the first in a series of modules around branding and packaging of products. The accompanying slides and workbook can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key words: Brand, branding, USP, unique selling proposition, brand value, market position, The Offices, purpose

Title: Production Scale-Up Q&A 08/02/2022
Content: Rob Munton from Food Projx Pty Ltd provided this Q&A session to discuss any questions resulting from his Production Scale-Up module. This Q&A was recorded on the 8th February 2022.

Title: Brand Strategy- Brand Position
Content:
Presented by The Offices, this module builds on the Foundations and explains how to position your brand so as to persuade customers and consumers to select your products over those of your competitors. A number of real-world examples are provided as well as demonstrations of the positioning tools provided. The workbook and accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key words: Brand, branding, market position, value, The Offices

Title: Brand Strategy- Brand Personality
Content:
Presented by The Offices, this third module builds on the Foundations and Position and explains how to bring your brand to life by giving it personality and a voice. A number of real-world examples are provided as well as demonstrations of the positioning tools provided. The workbook and accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key words: The Offices, brand personality, archetype, voice, tone

Title: Design

Title: Fermenting The Science
Content:
Fermenting the Science- Recorded as part of the Good Fermentations Take Time event held in Launceston on the 28th February 2023. Presentations from Prof. Tom Ross, Dr. Belinda Chapman and Dr. Julie Martyn explore some of the science behind fermentation and how this science can be practically applied to real world products. The accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Keeping Fermentations Safe – Prof. Tom Ross, CFSI, University of Tasmania

The One and the Many, Unique and Complex Fermentation Cultures – Dr. Belinda Chapman, Quantal Bioscience, Sydney

Culturing a Start-Up – Dr. Julie Martyn, Artisa Plant-Based Foods

Title: Case Studies In Creativity

The accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Title: Growing The Fermentation Ecosystem
Content:
Growing the Fermentation Ecosystem- Recorded as part of the Good Fermentations Take Time event held in Launceston on the 28th February 2023. Presentations from Dr. Belinda Chapman, Dr. Samantha Sawyer and Dr. Matthew Fielding explore some of the practical aspects of equipment and training required to undertake laboratory and commercial scale fermentation, and new technology developments that can be used evaluate the results from such fermentation processes. The accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Kitting-Out and Scaling-Up, A Fermentation Equipment Primer – Dr. Belinda Chapman, Quantal Bioscience, Sydney

Novel technologies Smelling and Tasting Your Fermentations For You – Dr. Samantha Sawyer, CFI and CFSI, University of Tasmania

Developing the Next Generation of Fermentation Heros – Dr. Matthew Fielding, University College, University of Tasmania

Title: Module 1 - Overview
Content:
Helen Banyard and James Easley from ‘The Good Stuff’ introduce the ‘Stop Yelling, Start Selling’ module which has the objectives of reflecting on your strategic foundations, acquiring and retaining your best customers, creating a structured communications and messaging campaign and ultimately optimising your marketing spend.  Helen leads you through their proprietary workflow to help you undertake this process yourself reducing the need to retain expensive external resources to develop a robust marketing plan for your business and products.

Key Words: Marketing, selling, brand, The Good Stuff, Helen Banyard, Miro, messaging, audience, campaign

Title: Module 2 - Strategic Audit: Nine in 90
Content:
In this module Helen Banyard from ‘The Good Stuff’ details the proprietary tool that they use to perform a strategic audit of your current situation, called the ‘Nine in 90’.

The objective of this audit is to gain a view of your current brand and marketing situation, evaluate the strength of the foundation, highlight any gaps and help you develop concepts to make your brand compelling.  The ultimate objective is to give you confidence in your plans before committing significant resources or spend.

Worksheets and Miro links can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Strategy, strategic, The Good Stuff, Helen Banyard, Miro, brand, marketing, audience, voice

Title: Module 3 - Audience Research
Content:
In this module Helen Banyard from ‘The Good Stuff’ describes the importance of really understanding your audience in a very specific way rather than a general population.  With this understanding you are best able to understand their problems and direct your messaging to their particular needs.

Worksheets can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Marketing, brand, audience, persona, Helen Banyard, The Good Stuff, survey, research

Title: Module 4 - Value Proposition and Brand Positioning
Content:
Following on from audience research in the previous module Helen from ‘The Good Stuff’ introduces the ideas of the value proposition and brand positioning and their importance in communication with your customers.  The module then describes some tools that allow you explore and create these concepts for your own brand, business and products.

Worksheets and Miro links can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Brand, marketing, position, value proposition, perception map

Title: Module 5 - Campaign
Content:
In the final module of the series, Helen Banyard from ‘The Good Stuff’ pulls together the outputs from the previous modules to generate an overall plan for your marketing campaign.  While the tools in previous modules will allow you to undertake most of the work yourselves it is often at the final campaign stage where you will decide to seek out expert professional help.  This module helps in the decision making of process around when, and how, to hand off the campaign to a professional agency, understanding what your own role should still be and how to evaluate the work generated by an external agent.

Worksheets and Miro links can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Marketing, agency, campaign, Helen Banyard, The Good Stuff, brief

Title: Value with Joseph Donovan
Content:
In this, the second of four sessions from Kantar, Joseph (Joey) Donovan presents his view of the current trends in FMCG (particularly food) around the actuality and perception of value. Joey is an Associate Director at Kantar Insights. This session was recorded on the 9th July 2024. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Some questions were taken on notice in the session and Joey has responded with these answers:-

  • What exactly is the reduction in "paying more for sustainability" related to": I think this is a hard one to tease out and it definitely is influenced by cost-of-living pressures being faced by everyone. However, I also looked into a stat from out GlobalMonitor data and found there is a higher proportion of GenZ (58%) who feel businesses/governments are more responsible for fixing environmental problems than themselves compared to younger millennials (50%) and especially middle age (43%). I don't have information on how this has shifted in the past few years though. However, today is seems there is a higher expectation that businesses should just naturally be doing good with the younger generation. 
  • Trends in personal care packaging: I loved this question, but I was fresh out of ideas. I did think of some other trends though over the last few days I have noticed
    • Packaging that limits waste: Solving a pain point of some personal care packaging (like bottles, squeezy tubes, rigid packaging with small openings, etc.) that always leave you with throwing some of the product out. People don't want to see things go to waste. I think this is where novel pump ideas (like HiSmile) or packaging with removeable interiors (think lotion where you can replace the inside container that holds the cream) come into play. From the frame of value, this is allowing a consumer to get more for their money (reduction in waste, but with a personal benefit, like the Woolies "Odd Bunch" example)
    • Mini/Travel: I have seen the mini space pick-up in this industry. You no longer need to use a B-grade product when you travel because that is all that's available in a small size. Like the Mini/Maxi trend we discussed, companies are making smaller sizes of their products available for when you are on the go. Mecca has a whole portion of their website dedicated to mini/travel!
    • Reuseable/Refillable: Again, a sustainability play, but it adds value for the consumer because refilling costs less. One brand I know that does this is Le Labo (you will have to go to the US website) but I am sure there are tons of others. I also think Sephora has a sustainability program with some cosmetics. (Note links to external sites provided).

Key Words: Trends, 2024, Kantar, insights, data, value, solutions, perception
